title: The Rim
name: The Rim
metaTitle: The Rim San Antonio 1031 Exchange Replacement Properties
metaDescription: 1031 exchange coordination for power-center and big-box retail property at The Rim along Loop 1604, adjacent to La Cantera on San Antonio's far northwest side.

sections.html: The Rim is a large power-center development along Loop 1604 immediately adjacent to La Cantera, combining big-box anchors, national restaurant pads, and a broad mix of mid-size retail tenants in a format distinct from La Cantera's more upscale lifestyle-center approach. For 1031 exchange buyers, The Rim offers a different retail profile within the same high-growth northwest San Antonio corridor.

sections.heading: Power-Center and Big-Box Retail Stock at The Rim
sections.html:
Big-box anchors and national mid-size retailers form the backbone of The Rim, supplemented by a dense collection of national restaurant and quick-service outparcels along the center's frontage. This format caters to value and convenience-driven shopping rather than the destination, upscale positioning of neighboring La Cantera, giving the center a broader, less discretionary-spending-dependent tenant mix.

These properties still satisfy the like-kind standard for real property held for investment or business use, so an investor exiting a different asset class elsewhere faces no classification issue identifying property here, and the center's broad national-tenant base tends to appeal to exchange buyers prioritizing tenant credit over destination cachet.

sections.heading: What Drives Demand at The Rim
sections.html: The Rim benefits from the same northwest San Antonio growth and household income levels that support neighboring La Cantera, but draws a broader, more value-oriented customer base given its big-box and power-center format. Loop 1604's continued traffic growth and the corridor's ongoing residential expansion further northwest keep pulling new rooftops into the center's trade area, and its outparcel restaurant pads benefit from the combined draw of both The Rim and La Cantera sitting side by side.

sections.heading: Closing Coordination for a Rim Property
sections.html:
Big-box and power-center leases often carry operating covenant and co-tenancy provisions tied to anchor performance, which need review before a property is placed on a written identification. Coordination for a Rim file typically covers:

  • Operating covenant and co-tenancy clause review for power-center inline and pad sites
  • Written identification meeting the unambiguous-description standard under Treasury Regulation 1.1031(k)-1
  • Lease abstract and tenant credit review for the specific property
  • Lender preflight against The Rim's power-center pricing and cap rate range
  • Exchange agreement and assignment documents routed to the qualified intermediary before funding

This tracks documentation and timeline; it is not a substitute for the investor's own legal or tax review.

sections.heading: Watchouts for Rim Investors
sections.html: Big-box anchor performance and any co-tenancy triggers matter more here than at a single-tenant NNN deal, since an anchor closure or downsizing can affect adjacent inline and pad tenant obligations. Investors should also confirm whether a target pad site's operating covenant restricts competing uses, since that can limit re-tenanting flexibility if the current tenant vacates before the lease term ends.

faqs.q: What kind of property is available at The Rim for a 1031 exchange?
faqs.a: Big-box anchor space, national mid-size retail, and a dense collection of national restaurant and quick-service outparcels along the center's Loop 1604 frontage, formatted as a value-oriented power center rather than an upscale lifestyle center.

faqs.q: How is The Rim different from neighboring La Cantera?
faqs.a: The Rim is a big-box and power-center format catering to value and convenience-driven shopping, while La Cantera is an upscale lifestyle center anchored by department stores and specialty retail, though both sit along the same Loop 1604 corridor.

faqs.q: What is a co-tenancy clause and why does it matter at The Rim?
faqs.a: It's a lease provision tying a tenant's rent or occupancy obligations to anchor tenant performance, which matters at power centers since an anchor closure or downsizing can trigger rent reductions or termination rights for adjacent tenants.

faqs.q: Should I check a pad site's operating covenant before identifying it?
faqs.a: Yes, operating covenants can restrict competing uses on nearby pads, which affects re-tenanting flexibility if the current tenant vacates before the lease term ends, so this is worth reviewing before finalizing an identification.

faqs.q: Does The Rim's proximity to La Cantera help its outparcel restaurant pads?
faqs.a: Yes, outparcel restaurant pads at The Rim benefit from the combined customer draw of both The Rim and La Cantera sitting side by side along the same stretch of Loop 1604.
